commit.message:
Fix: drop old compound index on DB connect and add retry logic

The old unique index on {storeId, userId} persists in MongoDB even after
schema change. Now drops it on 'connected' event (when DB is ready), uses
autoIndex:false to avoid race conditions, and retries invite generation
once on duplicate key error. Also logs detailed error info for debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Mypage: profile section, order status filter, review writing dialog,

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove native codegen stubs from libcot, set up libnative as external backend

Native codegen (aarch64, x86_64) lives in libnative — an external backend
like libclif. It receives CIR L2 MLIR bytecode and returns .o files via
C ABI. NO access to libcot internals.

libcot changes:
- Deleted empty aarch64/ stubs and x86_64/ stubs from codegen/
- Deleted codegen/aarch64.zig entry point (205 lines)
- codegen.zig: native backend paths → unreachable
- Added minimal aarch64_abi.zig + x86_64_dwarf.zig for LLVM/Dwarf needs

libnative changes:
- build.zig: compiles to libnative.a static library
- lib.zig: C ABI entry point (native_compile, native_free, native_version)
  matching libclif's pattern. Pipeline: MLIR bytes → parse → cir.L2 →
  Select → MIR → Assemble → .o bytes
- parseMlirToCirL2 and generateNative are TODO stubs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(rate-limit): catch Redis URL parse errors, fail open gracefully

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove manifests/profiles from repo, rename scripts, add auto-release pipeline

Manifests & profiles:
- Remove manifests/ from git (not needed for image builds; kept locally for cluster-ops)
- Remove profiles/ from git (generated at build time by build-uki.sh; kept locally)
- Delete outdated profiles/uki-nvgpu3.yaml, uki-nvgpu4.yaml locally
- Update .gitignore to cover entire manifests/ and profiles/ directories

Script renames (drop numeric prefixes):
- 00-setup-keys.sh  → setup-keys.sh
- 01-build-uki.sh   → build-uki.sh
- 02-build-usb-image.sh → build-usb-image.sh
- 09-build-nvgpu.sh → build-extensions.sh  (more descriptive name)
- Update Makefile, ci.yaml, and internal script calls accordingly

CI / Renovate auto-update pipeline:
- Add .github/workflows/auto-tag.yaml: triggers on main push when common.sh
  or pkg.yaml changes; reads TALOS_VERSION+NVGPU_VERSION, creates git tag
  v<talos>-nvgpu<nvgpu> → triggers release.yaml to build full USB image
- renovate.json: add LLVM_REV tracking (siderolabs/llvm releases), enable
  automerge for all Renovate PRs after CI passes, remove ignoreDeps for
  custom packages, update schedule to "at any time"
- release.yaml: add version table (Talos/kernel/nvgpu) to GitHub Release body
- ci.yaml: only shellcheck committed build scripts, validate workflow YAMLs
  and pkg.yaml (drop manifests/profiles validation since they're removed)

Flow: Renovate detects new Talos/kernel/LLVM version → opens PR on renovate/**
branch → CI runs (shellcheck + YAML validate) → Renovate automerges to main →
auto-tag.yaml creates versioned tag → release.yaml builds extensions + USB image
+ creates GitHub Release.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Move CIR L2 into stdlib: clean compiler/library split

CIR instruction format now lives in lib/std/cir/ — importable by
anyone without linking the compiler. Both libcot and libnative import
CIR from stdlib via the `cir` build module.

What moved to stdlib (lib/std/cir/):
- L2.zig: 2,151-line instruction format (Tag, Inst, Data, Ref, etc.)
- InternedIndex.zig: well-known interned constants (enum(u32))
- Alignment.zig: power-of-2 alignment type (enum(u6))
- Arc/Traits/Concurrency/Error/Tag: dialect types (were duplicated)

What stays in libcot (compiler-only):
- CirOps.zig: typeOf, mustLower, refToType, unwrapShuffle (need IP/Type)
- Cir/L2/: Legalize, Liveness, print passes
- Cir/L2.zig: 21-line re-export of stdlib + compiler passes

Key changes:
- InternPool.Index = std.cir.InternedIndex (same type, no copies)
- InternPool.Alignment = std.cir.Alignment (same type, no copies)
- Ref.toType/fromType/fromValue → CirOps free functions
- Data.ty: Type → InternedIndex (compiler wraps at boundary)
- build.zig: wires lib/std/cir.zig as `cir` module
- Deleted libcir/ (dead code) and all duplicate dialect files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Plan: move CIR into stdlib with clean compiler/library split

Detailed execution plan based on Swift (KnownStdlibTypes.def) and
MLIR (dialect definitions separate from compiler passes) patterns.

Key insight: L2.zig mixes data format (tags, structs, unwrap helpers)
with compiler operations (typeOf needing InternPool). Split them:
- lib/std/cir/L2.zig = pure data format (no compiler deps)
- libcot/CirOps.zig = compiler operations on CIR types
- Both libcot and libnative import std.cir.L2

6 execution steps with specific line-by-line guidance.

- .init username derived deterministically from address
- Gas deducted (~0.5 INIT) on each deploy

Contract Templates (6 total):
- Counter — beginner, increment/decrement/reset with owner auth
- CW20 Token — intermediate, fungible token with mint/burn/transfer
- CW721 NFT — intermediate, NFT collection with mint/transfer/burn
- Escrow — intermediate, time-locked with arbiter approval
- Staking — advanced, stake/unstake/claim with reward rate
- Voting/DAO — advanced, proposals with quorum and threshold

Template picker now shows difficulty badges (beginner/intermediate/advanced)
and category color coding. Counter is the default loaded contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Create libnative: native codegen from CIR L2

Copied from ~/claude/references/zig/src/codegen/ with Air→CirL2,
Zir→CirL1 rename applied. Same architecture-specific backends as the
Zig compiler (aarch64, x86_64) but using CIR naming.

Structure:
  libnative/
    lib.zig           — root entry point
    codegen.zig       — dispatch to architecture backends
    codegen/
      aarch64.zig     — ARM64 backend entry (205 lines)
      aarch64/        — Select, Mir, Assemble, encoding (34K lines)
      x86_64/         — x86-64 backend (CodeGen, Mir, Lower, Emit)

These files still import libcot types (InternPool, Type, Zcu) so
libnative is part of the same compilation unit for now.

Next: wire libnative into libcot's build so the native backend can
consume CIR L2 directly, bypassing MLIR/Cranelift for correct
handling of SRET, error unions, aggregates, etc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(extract): forward charon config from aeneas-config.yml, v0.5.0

Parse the `charon` section from aeneas-config.yml (cargo_args, start_from,
exclude, opaque, package, preset) and pre-generate the Charon LLBC file
with the full project-specific settings before running probe-rust.

Previously, probe-rust --with-charon ran charon with only --preset aeneas,
missing project-specific cargo args (e.g. --no-default-features --features
alloc,zeroize) and filter lists. This caused LLBC generation to silently
fail on projects like curve25519-dalek, falling back to heuristic
rust-qualified-names.

The generated LLBC is cached at <rust_project>/data/charon.llbc and reused
by probe-rust on subsequent runs.

Made-with: Cursor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add extraction step to bridge download-artifact v8 directory layout

(flat) instead of v4's {path}/{name}/{name}.zip (subdirectory per artifact).
Add an extraction step after download that unzips each blob into its named
subdirectory, restoring the v4 layout expected by downstream unzip steps.

commit.message:
feat: pivot CloseUp to Short-Form Video Content Engine (#13) (#13)

Redefine MVP from AI Interviewer (5-10 min conversation → highlight clip)
to Short-Form Video Content Engine (inspire → script → teleprompter →
AI highlight select + reorder → ~60s editorial video).

7 features across 3 sprints (15 days):
- Sprint 1: Schema migration + onboarding intro flow + full editorial video
- Sprint 2: Scraps system + API keys + main content record flow
- Sprint 3: LinkedIn/X OAuth posting + video history + npm skills package

Key changes:
- Rewrite all project-knowledge docs (product, architecture, integrations,
  data-model, gtm, validation, competitive, landing) for new direction
- Update CLAUDE.md files with new routes (/posts/*), stack, constraints
- Replace interview-specialist agent with script-specialist agent
- Delete superseded mvp/ docs (design, eng-plan, ceo-plan, test-plan,
  elevenlabs-system-prompt) — history preserved in git
- Add ideation design doc and deep interview implementation spec
- New schema: posts table replaces interviewSessions, plus scraps,
  apiKeys, socialConnections, socialPosts tables
- New integrations: LinkedIn OAuth, X API v2, CloseUp REST API for scraps

Design validated through /ideation (5 independent perspectives: VC,
Target User, Tech Advisor, Codex, Gemini) and /deep-interview (14 rounds,
5% ambiguity). Full specs at .omc/specs/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

SRET: struct return via hidden pointer, aggregate store as memcpy

Following Flang/FIR pattern (TargetRewrite.cpp):

Callee side:
- uses_sret detected for non-scalar returns (struct, error_union)
- Hidden first param added to function signature (i64 pointer)
- ret_ptr returns SRET pointer (block arg 0)
- ret_load copies from source to SRET then returns void
- arg instructions offset by 1 to skip SRET param

Call site:
- SRET calls allocate stack temp, pass as hidden first arg
- Call returns void, result is the SRET pointer

Aggregate store:
